title: Delegate professional secret scanning and preserve local fast guard
outOfScope: GitHub repository settings and enabling GitHub Secret Scanning

sources.reason: Current local secret scanner and supply-chain evidence command boundary.
Current CI quality and supply-chain gate execution point.
Review finding P1-4 requires delegated professional secret scanning evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: A delegated scanner must fail the CI evidence gate on findings or unavailable results while preserving a fast local guard.

executionDecision.reason: The delegated evidence boundary, CI integration point, and local regression scope are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that the external scan is pinned and fail-closed without treating the local lightweight scanner as complete historical secret coverage.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The smoke workflow runs a pinned professional secret scanner over the repository checkout and fails when the delegated scanner reports a finding or cannot produce a result.
The local lightweight scanner remains available through make check-secret-scanning and is documented as a fast guard rather than complete historical coverage.
CI exposes a named delegated secret-scanning check that is included in the release-blocking smoke evidence path.
Tests verify the workflow wiring, fail-closed delegated result handling, and preservation of the local scanner behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.
External security evidence must be produced by a pinned tool and must not be replaced by a custom scanner implementation.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: delegated scanner succeeds with no findings
delegated scanner is unavailable or returns malformed output
local lightweight scanner still detects fixture secrets

intent.problem: The repository's secret gate only uses a lightweight pattern scanner over the current checkout, so it cannot provide professional-tool coverage or historical scanning evidence for release decisions.
constraints: Keep the existing local scanner as a fast guard.
Do not add a second custom secret-scanner implementation.
Keep external tool execution pinned and fail closed in CI.
Do not publish a new version in this Work Item.
intent.rationale: AI Cockpit should govern evidence produced by specialized security tools rather than claim that its own pattern matching is complete secret detection.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed execution of the planned remediation loop, including CI and supply-chain evidence updates.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
